The Company faces intense competition from established and emerging competitors in all of its markets. Competitive pressures may require the Company to reduce prices, which could reduce gross margins and adversely affect financial results.

The Company's operations are subject to complex and evolving legal and regulatory requirements in the jurisdictions in which it operates. Failure to comply with applicable laws and regulations could subject the Company to civil or criminal penalties and other remedies.

The Company relies on third-party manufacturers for substantially all of its hardware products. These suppliers and manufacturers are primarily located in Asia. The Company's ability to offer products at competitive prices depends on its ability to maintain favorable relationships with its suppliers.

The Company is exposed to the risk of employee misconduct, including the misappropriation of confidential information and violations of applicable law. The Company maintains policies and procedures designed to deter, detect and address misconduct.

Changes in international trade policies, including tariffs and trade agreements, may adversely affect the Company's business. The Company sources many components and products from international suppliers, and changes in trade policy could increase costs or disrupt supply chains.

The Company's success depends on the continued service and performance of senior management and other key personnel. The loss of services of one or more of these individuals could adversely affect the Company's operations and strategic direction.
